paper_title: Study of L. aestivum Populations in Po Valley

methods.description: This method describes the process of capturing and identifying arthropods visiting or found near the flowers of L. aestivum in different study populations during the flowering period.

steps.description: We visited the study populations during the flowering period in April-May twice a week; we captured all arthropods visiting or found near (i.e. a few cm) the flowers of L. aestivum with an entomological net or directly with plastic test tubes.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture arthropods near L. aestivum flowers using nets or test tubes

steps.description: The tubes containing the entomological samples were dated and coded.
In detail, in each day, five 30-min sampling sessions were performed in the morning (10 am), at noon (12 am), in the afternoon (3 pm), at twilight (8 pm) and at night (9 pm).
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct five 30-minute sampling sessions at specified times

steps.description: Temperature during the sampling sessions were measured by using a DataLogger Geoprecision at 10' intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure temperature during sampling sessions using DataLogger

steps.description: All collected samples were placed in plastic tubes and frozen waiting to be identified at MUSE-Science Museum of Trento (MUSE).
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze samples for later identification at MUSE

steps.description: About 70% of the caught specimens was identified to the species level, ca. 8% to the genus level, the rest to higher taxonomic levels (family or order). Specimens were identified by using entomological collections available at MUSE and thanks to the cooperation of skilled taxonomists.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify specimens to species, genus, or higher taxonomic levels using MUSE collections
